name: data-structures
context: Redis data structures: strings, lists, sets, sorted sets, hashes, bitmaps, HyperLogLog
info: This reference covers the full range of Redis data structures beyond simple key-value caching. Strings support atomic counters (INCR, DECR) and bitwise operations. Lists provide O(1) push/pop for queues and stacks. Sets offer membership testing, intersection, union, and difference. Sorted sets add a score to each member for ranking, range queries, and leaderboards. Hashes store field-value pairs within a single key for object-like storage. Bitmaps enable compact boolean arrays for feature flags and daily active user tracking. HyperLogLog provides probabilistic cardinality estimation with constant memory. Each structure has dedicated commands optimized for its access patterns.
triggers: what redis data types are available
use redis as a queue with lists
use redis bitmaps for feature flags

sections.title: Strings and counters
sections.body: Strings hold values up to 512MB. Beyond GET/SET (see [[redis/caching#get-set]]), use INCR/DECR for atomic counters, APPEND for log-style writes, and GETRANGE/SETRANGE for substrings. Strings are also the backing type for bitmaps. Most caching workloads use strings as their primary type.

sections.title: Lists
sections.body: Doubly-linked lists with O(1) push/pop at both ends. LPUSH/RPOP for FIFO queues, LPUSH/LPOP for LIFO stacks. BRPOP/BLPOP for blocking consumer patterns (worker queues). LRANGE for pagination. LTRIM to cap list length (e.g., recent activity feeds). For durable queue semantics with acknowledgment, consider [[redis/streams]] instead.

sections.title: Sets and sorted sets
sections.body: Sets store unique members with O(1) add/remove/membership. SINTER, SUNION, SDIFF for set algebra. Sorted sets add a numeric score to each member. ZADD/ZRANGE/ZREVRANGE for leaderboards. ZRANGEBYSCORE for range queries. ZINCRBY for score updates. Sorted sets enable rate limiting via sliding window (ZADD + ZREMRANGEBYSCORE + ZCARD). See [[redis/caching]] for using sorted sets as scored caches.

sections.title: Hashes
sections.body: Hashes store field-value pairs within a single key, ideal for object storage without serialization. HSET/HGET for individual fields, HGETALL for the entire object, HINCRBY for atomic field increments. More memory-efficient than storing each field as a separate string key. Useful for user profiles, sessions, and configuration objects.

sections.title: Bitmaps and HyperLogLog
sections.body: Bitmaps use SETBIT/GETBIT to manipulate individual bits within a string. BITCOUNT for population count, BITOP for AND/OR/XOR across keys. Ideal for feature flags and daily active user tracking (1 bit per user). HyperLogLog uses PFADD/PFCOUNT for probabilistic unique counting with ~12KB constant memory. PFMERGE combines multiple HLLs. Perfect for unique visitor counts and cardinality estimation where exact counts are not required.
